Name: cuda_kernel_optimization
Title: Optimizing CUDA Kernels for Deep Learning Workloads: A Hardware-Centric Approach
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing CUDA kernels for memory-bandwidth utilization and tensor-core occupancy, we can achieve significant speedups in deep learning workloads on commodity GPUs.
Related Work: While previous work has focused on optimizing deep learning models for specific hardware platforms, our approach focuses on optimizing the CUDA kernels themselves, taking into account the unique characteristics of the hardware. This approach is distinct from previous work in that it prioritizes hardware-centric optimization over model-centric optimization.
Abstract: In this work, we propose a hardware-centric approach to optimizing deep learning workloads on commodity GPUs. By optimizing CUDA kernels for memory-bandwidth utilization and tensor-core occupancy, we demonstrate significant speedups in deep learning workloads. Our approach prioritizes hardware-faithful evaluation, using tools such as nvprof and Nsight to measure kernel performance and attribute gains to specific hardware causes. We evaluate our approach on a range of deep learning models and demonstrate reproducible benchmarks, reporting p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments on a range of deep learning models, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and recurrent neural networks (RNNs). We will optimize CUDA kernels for memory-bandwidth utilization and tensor-core occupancy, and evaluate the performance of the optimized kernels using nvprof and Nsight. We will report p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token, and attribute gains to specific hardware causes using tools such as nvprof and Nsight.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The main risk factor for this proposal is that the optimization of CUDA kernels may not translate to significant speedups in deep learning workloads. Additionally, the approach may be sensitive to the specific hardware platform used, and may not generalize to other platforms. To mitigate these risks, we will conduct thorough experiments on a range of deep learning models and hardware platforms, and report detailed results using tools such as nvprof and Nsight.
